commit.message:
fix: upgrade Go toolchain to 1.26.2 to resolve stdlib vulnerabilities (#276)

## Summary
- Bumps `go.mod` from `go 1.26.1` to `go 1.26.2` to resolve 5 Go
standard library vulnerabilities
- `govulncheck` now reports 0 vulnerabilities (down from 5)
- Build and `go vet` pass cleanly with go1.26.2

## Vulnerabilities Fixed

| ID | Package | Description |
|----|---------|-------------|
| GO-2026-4947 | `crypto/x509` | Unexpected work during chain building |
| GO-2026-4946 | `crypto/x509` | Inefficient policy validation |
| GO-2026-4870 | `crypto/tls` | Unauthenticated TLS 1.3 KeyUpdate causes
DoS |
| GO-2026-4866 | `crypto/x509` | Case-sensitive excludedSubtrees name
constraints auth bypass |
| GO-2026-4865 | `html/template` | JsBraceDepth context tracking bugs
(XSS) |

## Changes
- `go-api/go.mod`: `go 1.26.1` → `go 1.26.2` (1-line change)

## Validation
- `govulncheck ./...`: 0 vulnerabilities
- `go build ./...`: pass
- `go vet ./...`: pass

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)


## Follow-up commit
- Switched CI Go setup from \`go-version: '1.26'\` to \`go-version-file:
go-api/go.mod\` per Copilot review feedback, so the exact patched
toolchain is always used.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add AuthRepository scaffold and refactor pgsql.DB to OpenDB function (#12)

* Add AuthRepository scaffold and refactor pgsql.DB to OpenDB function

Replace the pgsql.DB wrapper struct with a single OpenDB function that
returns *sql.DB directly. The wrapper only existed for lifecycle
management (pool config + open/close), but callers needed the underlying
*sql.DB anyway — for repository construction and transactions. This is
the conventional Go pattern (used by sqlx, GORM, and recommended by
Alex Edwards' Let's Go Further).

Also adds the AuthRepository struct and constructor that accepts *sql.DB.
Query methods are implemented in follow-up PRs (6b–6e); the compile-time
interface check is added in 6e once all methods exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update internal/pgsql/pgsql_test.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: provider-agnostic AI + smart continuous screen watch

- Switch AI client from Azure-specific SDK to openai npm package
  (any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via configurable baseURL)
- Remove Azure AI branding from settings, env vars renamed:
  azureAiEndpoint/Key/Model -> aiEndpoint/aiKey/aiModel
- Add screen-watcher.ts: continuous 10s screenshot analysis with
  two-stage smart filter:
  1. Fast classifier call: detects QUIZ, PRESENTATION, WHITEBOARD,
     DOCUMENT, CODE, GRAPH, FORM vs skippable DESKTOP/VIDEO/CHAT
  2. Full analysis only for noteworthy content (confidence >= 60%)
  3. Image hash dedup: skip unchanged screens and re-analyzed slides
  4. Auto-saves note tagged with detected category
- Add shared-state.ts for transcript buffer (used by IPC + watcher)
- screenWatchEnabled toggle in Settings panel
- Purple watch-dot indicator in header when watching
- Status toast when auto-note is captured

commit.message:
Add dot product dilution insight to high-dimension limitations

Explains that while the full PE vector is unique, the Q/K dot product
is a sum of per-dimension contributions — near-zero positional
differences in high-i dimensions dilute the signal from useful
low-i dimensions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix inaccurate gradient claim in high-dimension limitations

Replaced misleading 'gradients are minimal' with accurate explanation:
PE has no learnable params, so the real issue is lack of positional
variation in the input to Q/K/V projections, not gradient magnitude.

refactor: inline orchestrators into callers, rename webhook → gitlab_webhook (Phase 6.2) (#392)

Eliminate orchestrator.py, discussion_orchestrator.py, and
coding_orchestrator.py by inlining their logic into the callers:

- gitlab_webhook.py (renamed from webhook.py): inline tracer span,
  GitLabClient creation, pipeline construction, and run_pipeline
  for both review and discussion paths
- gitlab_poller.py: same inlining for _process_mr and _process_notes
- coding_pipeline.py: add CodingTaskRunner (replaces CodingOrchestrator),
  satisfies CodingTaskHandler protocol with trace span + repo lock +
  pipeline execution (dedup stays in JiraPoller)

Test updates:
- Replace orchestrator function mocks with run_pipeline mocks
- Construct pipelines directly in integration/metrics tests
- Add explicit repo lock regression tests for webhook and poller
- Rename test_discussion_orchestrator.py → test_discussion_pipeline.py

Documentation updated across wiki, ADRs, and sequence diagrams.

commit.message:
fix: show Device Offline badge instead of stuck Starting on dashboard (#153) (#162)

When a schedule is active but the target device is offline, the dashboard
previously showed a perpetual 'Starting...' badge (in both Now Playing and
Coming Up sections). Now it correctly shows a 'Device Offline' warning badge.

Changes:
- scheduler.py: get_upcoming_schedules() accepts offline_device_ids param;
  marks entries with device_offline=True instead of starting=True
- ui.py: Both HTML and JSON dashboard handlers check device connectivity
  and set device_offline flag on now_playing entries (grace period and post)
- dashboard.html: New badge-offline rendering in Now Playing and Coming Up;
  fingerprint includes device_offline for proper change detection
- style.css: badge-offline style (warning/yellow)
- 6 new tests covering offline badge in HTML/JSON, grace period behavior,
  offline-to-online transition, and scheduler-level offline detection

Closes #153

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: smooth image gallery swipe and page transition animations (#208)

- Fix root cause of flash/pop in gallery: remove CSS opacity classes that
  conflict with framer-motion inline-style opacity (inline wins, making
  the class-based system a no-op; image content popped in after slide
  animation). Framer-motion now exclusively owns opacity.
- Animate skeleton fade-out via AnimatePresence + Motion.div exit so the
  loading state dissolves smoothly rather than vanishing instantly.
- Add will-change: transform, opacity to gallery img, lightbox img, and
  lightbox inner container for GPU layer promotion.
- Tune gallery spring: stiffness 380→340, damping 38→44, mass 0.8→0.75
  (removes overshoot, keeps slide crisp). Exit travel reduced to 80% so
  the outgoing image clears faster. Opacity ease → material [0.4,0,0.2,1].
- Unify PageTransition enter easing with ProductModal ([0.22,1,0.36,1]),
  duration 0.25→0.28s, exit ease → [0.36,0,0.66,0]. Drift 10→12 / 6→8px.
- ProductModal backdrop: add ease [0.4,0,0.2,1]. Panel: add will-change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Expand cards with multiple TCGPlayer variants into separate checklist entries (#21)

* Expand cards with multiple TCGPlayer variants into separate entries

Previously, one TCG API card mapped to one checklist entry. But
many cards have multiple TCGPlayer price types (normal + reverseHolofoil,
holofoil + reverseHolofoil, 1stEditionHolofoil + unlimitedHolofoil).
For a master set, each variant needs its own checklist entry.

Now mapTCGCardToCards() expands a single API card into multiple
PokemonCard entries — one per price type variant. Each gets a unique
ID (e.g., ex7-67-reverseHolofoil), correct variant type, variant
label, and individual pricing.

This fixes missing entries for:
- Reverse holo / stamped variants (e.g., EX Team Rocket Returns Mareep)
- 1st Edition vs Unlimited printings (e.g., Neo Destiny Dark Ampharos)
- Normal vs Holofoil versions

Cameo cards are not expanded (one entry per cameo appearance).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: preserve semantic variant (promo/collab/etc) for expanded cards

Review feedback: mapVariant() was bypassed for cards with multiple
price types, losing promo/collab/tournament/secret-rare/etc variant
classification. Now uses mapVariant() as base variant and only falls
back to physical variant (holo/reverse-holo) when the card has no
more specific classification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate from PyRef/PyRefMut to PyClassGuard/PyClassGuardMut

PyClassGuard is the designated successor to PyRef in pyo3
(see PyO3/pyo3#2300).

Method receivers in DictProxy, ListProxy, and ScalarProxy now use
PyClassGuard/PyClassGuardMut. Since PyClassGuard does not carry a
Python<'py> token, methods that relied on self_.py() now take an
explicit py: Python<'_> parameter.

The TomlSource enum in dict_ops.rs previously stored a PyRef to keep
a source document borrowed across function boundaries. Since
PyClassGuard borrows rather than owns, this was restructured: TomlSource
now stores Py<Document> (refcount only, no data clone) and borrows on
demand via Py::extract::<PyClassGuard>() inside a with_item callback.
This also simplifies the API by removing the <'py> lifetime parameter
from TomlSource, ResolvedUpdate, and resolve_update.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: guard against exogenous train/future mismatch in MLForecast

If X_df was present for fit() but Xf_df was None for predict() (or
vice versa), mlforecast would raise a cryptic ValueError at runtime.
Add an explicit guard with a clear error message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mt-data-mcp
repository.description: A MCP server for MetaTrader5 that provides market data and technical indicators

commit.message:
forecast: reject unknown transform instead of silent fallback

_RECONSTRUCTION_MODES.get() used _inverse_log_return as fallback for
unrecognized transform keys, silently applying log-return inversion
to data that may use a different transform. Replace with None default
and return None (reconstruction failure) with a warning log.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: manifest-driven AI kit integration + project-tooling model (#3)

* feat: manifest-driven AI kit integration + project-tooling model

- Add generic linker: .specify/scripts/bash/link-ai-integration.sh
  - Reads project-tooling.json to honor spec_workflow: false
  - Skips speckit.* assets when spec_workflow is disabled
  - Writes/merges project-tooling.json after linking

- Add project-tooling.json generation in install.sh (step 5)
  - Seeds spec_workflow: true, ai_tool: null for consumer declaration

- Bundle .specify/extensions/* and .specify/integrations/copilot/scripts
  - Copilot integration adapter scripts tracked as source content
  - Integration install receipts (*.manifest.json) gitignored

- Add docs/integrations.md documenting ai-kit.manifest.json contract,
  project-tooling.json schema, and separation model

- Remove root README.md (docs/ is the docs root for this repo)
- Remove extensions/manifest.json, presets/personal.json (unused)
- Update docs/README.md, docs/extensions.md, docs/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Update .gitignore: ignore integrations/*.manifest.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove github-actions dependabot config (no workflows exist)

Dependabot was failing with dependency_file_not_found because no
.github/workflows/*.yml files exist in speckit-core. Clear the
updates list until workflows are added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove dependabot.yml (no dependencies to track)

An empty updates array is invalid per the dependabot schema.
speckit-core has no package ecosystem to update, so remove
the file entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.1.0: Major design improvements from rubber-duck review

- Error hierarchy: ActionGraphQLError, ActionAuthError,
  ActionRateLimitError, ActionTransportError (all exported)
- Consistent return types: all methods unwrap GraphQL root keys
  (e.g. get_current_user returns user dict, not {currentUser: ...})
- Configurable timeout (default 30s), max_retries, backoff_factor
- Auto-refresh on 401: retries once after refreshing token
- Product search pagination: added page parameter
- search_products returns flattened {products: [...], pagination: {...}}
- Locale validation: warns instead of raising on unknown locales
- Explorer loads query catalog from /api/queries (shared with queries.py)
- Version set to 0.1.0 (pre-1.0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
regime: apply min_regime_bars smoothing to clustering method

HMM and MS-AR paths called _smooth_short_state_runs to merge short
regime runs, but the clustering (KMeans) path skipped it entirely.
This caused min_regime_bars to be silently ignored for method=clustering.

Fix: smooth and canonicalize labels on the valid slice only (preserving
the leading -1 undefined window), then map back to full length.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
trading: normalize position ticket resolution fields

Success path used singular 'attempt' and omitted 'matched' field,
while failure path used plural 'attempts' and included 'matched'.
Normalize both paths to always emit 'attempts' (int) and 'matched'
(bool) for consistent downstream consumption.

commit.message:
services: close HTTP response objects in Yahoo options retry loop

In _yahoo_http_get(), the response was not closed before retrying,
leaking connections on 429/5xx retries. In _fetch_yahoo_options_payload(),
the response was not closed if raise_for_status() or json() raised.

Fix: close response before each retry iteration, and wrap the caller's
response lifecycle in try/finally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 1: SEC filings tool, macro regime agent, earnings interpreter (#33)

* Phase 1: SEC filings tool, macro regime agent, earnings interpreter

SEC filings MCP tool (sec-filings.ts):
- Ticker-to-CIK resolution via SEC company_tickers.json
- EDGAR submissions API for filing listing
- Full filing text fetch with HTML stripping
- Section extraction (risk-factors, mda, business, financials, legal)

Macro regime agent (macro_regime.py):
- FRED economic indicator fetching (GDP, UNRATE, CPI, etc.)
- Period-over-period change calc with Decimal arithmetic
- Regime classification (EXPANSION/CONTRACTION/TRANSITION)
- Dashboard formatting with indicator readings

Earnings interpreter agent (earnings_interpreter.py):
- Sentiment scoring with earnings-tuned lexicons
- Transcript splitting (prepared remarks vs Q&A)
- Tone shift detection between sections
- Guidance extraction and classification (raised/lowered/maintained)
- Management confidence via hedging ratio

Also updates copilot instructions with issue-first workflow requirement.

Closes #30, Closes #31, Closes #32

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Switch build backend from setuptools to hatchling

setuptools >=82 requires PEP 660 support that older pip versions
lack. hatchling has native editable install support across all
pip versions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README and copilot instructions for Python venv workflow

Ubuntu 24.04 enforces PEP 668 — system Python packages are
externally managed. All Python work requires a venv.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pytest failures and ruff lint errors

- Fix guidance regex to handle words between 'raising' and 'guidance'
- Remove extraneous f-string prefixes and unused imports
- Wrap long lines under 100 chars (source + tests)
- Add _reading() helper in tests to reduce IndicatorReading boilerplate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add fuzzy source_id dedup to prevent duplicate suggested tasks

- Add normalize_source_id() to parse source_ids into (type, person_alias, tokens)
- Add find_similar_source() for Jaccard similarity matching on keyword tokens
- Wire into create_task(): exact source_id match first, fuzzy fallback second
- Person part normalized by stripping email domain; keywords compared via Jaccard > 0.5
- Fuzzy matches are logged for observability
- Manual tasks (no source_id) are unaffected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
